REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN GOOD FAITH (1) THAT INSTRUCTIONS PER CCP 2943.1(D) TO CLOSE ESCROW WERE PROVIDED TO THE MOVING DEFENDANT; WHO PROVIDED THOSE INSTRUCTIONS AND WHO WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING THOSE INSTRUCTIONS BEFORE CLOSE OF ESCROW/DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS TO THE SELLER; AND/OR (2) THAT THE MOVING DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE TITLE TRANSFER OF THE PROPERTY AFTER SALE WAS FINALIZED, ESCROW CLOSED AND TITLE WAS RECORDED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Matthew S. Henderson; Parker Ibrahim & Berg LLP; 714-361-9550; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. (erroneously sued as "J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.).
Angelo A. DuPlantier; Crafts Law Firm; 949-484-7404; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of plaintiffs Zachary Samuel Bernstein and Kristen Rose Virdone.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' counsel requests that if the court adopts the tentative ruling, that plaintiffs be granted 30 days leave to amend. Defendant's counsel did not object to plaintiffs' request. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with 30 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in good faith (1) that instructions per CCP 2943.1(d) to close escrow were provided to the moving defendant; who provided those instructions and who was responsible for providing those instructions before close of escrow/disbursement of funds to the seller; and/or (2) that the moving defendant had actual notice of the title transfer of the property after sale was finalized, escrow closed and title was recorded. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
